title: The Curse of Canonicity Does NME Influence NGE Split
display_title: The Curse of Canonicity Does NME Influence NGE Split
wikitext:
[]: The Curse of Canonicity: Does NME Influence NGE? [Split]             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jun 19, 2013 5:19 pm [Split from "Is the curse of the Eva present in EoE?" - Monk] This brings up the wider issue of, not so much ret conning due to Rebuild, but rather add conning. Regarded as a Third Tier source of canon, Rebuild can potentially add material to the NGE universe if it is not in contradiction with what is seen in the show. Material like the Eva's blood type, Shinji's Helltrain bag, and Kaworu as the first angel are references which relate directly to concepts only alluded to in NGE, and as such it's difficult to ignore them. The problem is, how do we decide exactly what should be considered a valid "add-con" and what should not? While Kaworu stating that he was the First Angel in 3.0 seems like a straightforward addition, it should be remembered that the direct connection between Kaworu and Adam was only made in the Director's cut episodes and EoE. In the originally broadcast series, Kaworu was simply the 17th Angel, albiet one with a extra twist. This was actually a point of some contention among some analysts in the early days if I remember my Eva-lore correctly. But as a third tier source, Rebuild is at least as valid and valuable as the Manga, so we can't simply dismiss everything in it outright. Inferences about the Curse of Eva seem on the dubious end of the acceptance scale, but be aware that we as yet have no firm guidelines on what material in Rebuild we can accept, reject, or extend upon in NGE. And as for extending NGE material into Rebuild.... that remains an entirely undecided domain.

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Wed Jun 19, 2013 11:22 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: In the originally broadcast series, Kaworu was simply the 17th Angel, albiet one with a extra twist. This was actually a point of some contention among some analysts in the early days if I remember my Eva-lore correctly.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Jun 19, 2013 11:33 pm Didn't Misato refer to the " Curse of Eva" in the tv series? Though I think she was referring to the general misfortune that follows anybody involved with the Eva. Personally I'm not sure how much of the rebuild cannon we can apply to the original NGE. I'd like to wait unlit rebuild is over before we start applying anything to the original's cannon.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 20, 2013 9:30 am Asuka's curse appears to be related to her lack of aging. Appears mind you. Th3Marauder wrote: Oh really? How so?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jun 20, 2013 12:19 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: This brings up the wider issue of, not so much ret conning due to Rebuild, but rather add conning. Regarded as a Third Tier source of canon, Rebuild can potentially add material to the NGE universe if it is not in contradiction with what is seen in the show. Material like the Eva's blood type, Shinji's Helltrain bag, and Kaworu as the first angel are references which relate directly to concepts only alluded to in NGE, and as such it's difficult to ignore them.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jun 20, 2013 12:47 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: This brings up the wider issue of, not so much ret conning due to Rebuild, but rather add conning.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jun 20, 2013 1:01 pm NemZ wrote: I agree concerning the wiki, though the conclusion I reach from this is the exact opposite of yours; we should expect strict segregation of ALL continuities.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu Jun 20, 2013 1:13 pm Additionally, the exact particulars of the curse itself are unclear, so we shouldn't start attributing stuff to it for no reason. As stated earlier, though, Shinji has only been around for a couple of weeks at most, and Asuka only just reappeared, so they probably aren't suffering from immortality or anything.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 20, 2013 3:49 pm If anyone, anyone at all feels that Rebuild characters need their own seperate pages, then by all means log on to the wiki and make them! All interest in Wiki editing is welcome. Kendrix wrote: At such I'm boggled by the need of some ppl to "separate" everything Rebuild-related, since we already have the manga as an example for a semi-official, but non-identical continuity with explicit divergences, but based on the same source material/pool of ideas and providing the occasional clarification on it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jun 20, 2013 5:06 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Never mind the Manga. Consider NGE2 and the CI. The CI has practically become first tier canon, despite technically originating from a separate continuity. There's something to be said for the quality of additional information that another source offers.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jun 20, 2013 5:51 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Prior to Rebuild, I argued that the helltrain could have represented the young Shinji's lonely journey immediately after Gendo rejected him at the train station. It was a very tentative suggestion. But the addition of the bag in Rebuild moves an NGE suggestion up to the realm of very definite possibility -- almost a certainty in my opinion; the director has returned to the image and more explicitly conveyed the original message.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Jun 20, 2013 6:33 pm NemZ wrote: ...going by the wiki article on canonicity wouldn't it be 4th tier at best, and arguably even lower since at least the manga was specifically started with the same original plan?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jun 21, 2013 5:47 am Tiers of canon? Really? (Yes, I've been aware of it for a while, now.) Here's the thing: - I don't include deleted storyboards, alternate script drafts, or Wolverine Asuka in any Eva TV canon. On one hand they're fun little factoids, on the other hand they're more or less useless information if one isn't interested in watching the creative process at work with Eva. - I don't include much (if any) of Sadamoto manga, Angelic Days, or any other manga or video game elements into Eva TV canon. - I only use creators' comments and interviews of the Eva TV show as a litmus test to see if the filmmakers conveyed exactly what it was they meant to convey. And seeing as how we're dealing people that are more competent than Tommy Wiseau, I think it's a fair and safe way to handle that kind of information. - And I certainly don't project one Eva series to another Eva series. NME, NGE, or otherwise. Heck, it really only makes sense to do that in EoTV vs EoE discussions, and that's only because it's debatable as to whether or not those are separate canons to begin with. (And it's the debate that makes part of discussing Eva's conclusion fun.) See, I own both the 1989 Batman and the 2008 Batman Begins , and I have read both The Killing Joke and Batman: Year One, graphic novels that respectively helped shape the look and tone of the films. I don't add details from comic book to film, I don't add details from comic book to comic book, and I don't add details from film to film, as they all seem to be separate canons anyway and the stories themselves seem to stands well enough alone from the others to make this method work. If Nolan thought that a "Year One" story element was important in anyway to the story of his Batman movie, then he would have put it in there. If he didn't think it was important, than it was omitted. Same thing goes here. I guess one can argue that thematically "The Curse of Eva" can work in NGE, because Shinji doesn't seem to want to grow up and it would provide an interesting foil to Soryu's tendency to want to be an adult so quickly. It would also be interesting to see one-legged Touji be stuck in his teenage body while Hikari grew up to be a mature woman, and how that would effect their relationship. (Actually, Eva's Curse would work great in NGE for thematics alone!) But seeing as how it would only work on a thematic level in NGE, and none of the themes or motifs (or plot lines or story development or world building) in NGE reference this, then arguing that Eva's Curse is somehow in NGE ultimately ends up being a point that can't be proven. The fact that NGE doesn't spend enough time in the chronology of the story is simply the cherry on top. Even if Ritsuko says something in NGE like "The harmonics are effecting the LCL, causing the pilots to never age," or some other expository techno-babble like that, the fact that the show takes place over the course of a few months makes that point useless. If it's shoe-horned in NGE like that, nothing is gained. If it's removed from NGE all together, nothing is lost. Basically NGE would also have to pull a time skip of some sort in order to make that idea even present in the series; Gunbuster  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jun 21, 2013 5:54 am Good godless, FFF4E, what's the point of a spoiler box if you don't give any indication as to what it's a spoiler for, or that it's even an actual spoiler and not just a box to hide some witticism? Luckily, I already knew that about that series and will probably forget it again anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jun 21, 2013 6:00 am Sorry. I simply forgot. It's been fixed.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Fri Jun 21, 2013 1:56 pm Rebuild is clearly a seperate work, and it cannot add anything that was not in the original to the original. Perhaps the way things are done in Rebuild can clarify what was meant in the original version, as Math showed with the Shinji's bag thing, but nothing more. The same goes for the manga and whatever. The CI, on the other hand, is considered by many to be an extension of the original work. If it is indeed that, then it could obviously could provide information.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jun 21, 2013 2:35 pm Lavinius wrote: The CI, on the other hand, is an extension of the original work.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 21, 2013 4:31 pm The general consensus seems to be that Rebuild is a totally separate work. Perhaps the inherent bias of finding that Rebuild actually agrees with one of my wilder speculation has thrown me off, but I'd still regard Rebuild as being at least a potential source of third tier canon for NGE. But I'm torn here. My general philosophy has always been closer to FreakyFilmFan4ever's: Stick to First tier canon, make your arguments based on this, go down the tiers only when things get really speculative, technobabblish, or you get curious. But it's very hard to do this with Rebuild(It's particularly hard to think about writing Wiki articles with this mindset). Chuckman wrote: That's exactly what it means (and it's insightful- I never considered that) and you don't need some kind of external justification for your interpretation. If that's what you see in the work. it's as valid as any other.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jun 21, 2013 5:21 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Should we ignore even simple things like the new information in the Nerv readouts, set in the very same scene(A lot of the things I'm thinking about are technobabblish by the way, but everything needs to be considered).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jun 21, 2013 5:39 pm The CI material in almost all cases merely confirms what the series had all but spelled out; the only bit that was not readily inferred from the series was the soul-splitting in Kyoko's contact experiment.
